summary: Frontend E2E smoke of the 8 requested Ethara.AI flows using Playwright against the preview URL. All 47 assertions passed across CTO new-project, R&D budget-builder, TPM budget-builder, CTO budget-review-workspace, and TPM returned-budgets widget → edit & resubmit round trip.

issues: Recharts warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' fires repeatedly on mount for dashboard charts (cosmetic — charts still render on next tick). Consider adding minWidth/minHeight or a debounced resize observer to silence noise in console.

React DevTools hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — third-party dev instrumentation wraps <option> children with a <span data-ve-dynamic> which is invalid HTML. Not caused by app code but produced by the visual-editor overlay in preview; still logs a red console error.

action_items: (Optional) Silence Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warnings by giving chart containers a defined min-height and using ResponsiveContainer with debounce=1.
No functional fixes required — all 8 review-request flows behave as specified.
critical_code_review_comments: AppContext.jsx is ~620 lines and mixes session, projects, task logs, top-ups, budgets, batch deliveries, and budget reviews. Consider splitting into feature-scoped slices (contexts/hooks) for maintainability.

BudgetBuilder.jsx is ~780 lines with three tab bodies, stepper, prefill logic, and helper components in one file. Extracting ModelRow / InfraRow / SubRow / Stepper into their own files would improve readability and unit-test surface.

In BudgetReviewWorkspace.jsx the review is resolved from static BUDGET_REVIEWS (mockTpm) instead of merging with the dynamically-produced budgetReviews array; if a review only exists in localStorage (e.g. after a fresh submitBudget), the workspace falls back to BUDGET_REVIEWS[0]. Consider merging sources before lookup.

AppContext.visibleProjects filter treats R&D users as: 'rndMembers includes user.name OR p.tpm === user.name'. Second branch means an R&D user who happens to be a TPM on a project sees Production projects too — verify this is intended.

ctoReturnBudgetReview overwrites modifiedPhases/modifiedTotal with empty defaults; if CTO had edited the phase-wise breakdown before clicking Return, that work is discarded (only ctoComment is preserved). Consider capturing the current phaseTotals in the returned entry so R&D/TPM sees exactly what the CTO wanted.
test_credentials: cto@ethara.ai / demo123 · tpm@ethara.ai / demo123 · rd@ethara.ai / demo123 (all quick-login cards on /login).
seed_data_creation: During test run: (a) CTO created project TEST-Nova with R&D=Neha Kapoor (localStorage: ethara.customProjects.v1). (b) CTO returned br1 to TPM with a TEST comment (localStorage: ethara.budgetReviews.v1). Reset both keys via localStorage.clear() to restore preview baseline.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 8 review flows verified end-to-end in a single Playwright session (state carries via localStorage). Key data-testids validated: input-client-project-name, input-internal-name, input-doc-url, input-tpm, rnd-multi-picker, rnd-toggle-u9, btn-create-project, bb-budget-type (disabled for R&D), bb-delivery-single/multiple, bb-single-tasks, bb-single-trajectories, bb-doc-url, bb-tab-models/infra/subs, bb-infra-monthly-*, bb-sub-members-*, bb-sub-<row>-member-*, tab-overview, tab-modify (no phase/task/audit/comparison tabs), btn-save-draft, btn-return-tpm, btn-reject, btn-approve-forward, input-comment, widget-returned-budgets, returned-budget-br1, bb-returned-banner, bb-submit ('Resubmit budget' text when ?edit=<id>). Empty-comment guard on Return-to-TPM works. If retesting is needed, `localStorage.clear()` before starting to avoid seeded test state.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found relative to the review-request spec.
